intro: Let's check the federal and Ontario credits that apply to your situation. Many of these are easy to miss — especially the Ontario-specific credits that are often overlooked.

screens.title: Which of the following applied to you in 2025?
screens.instruction: Select all that apply. We'll collect the details we need based on your selections.

options.label: I paid rent for a principal residence in Ontario
I paid property tax directly on an Ontario principal residence
I am 64 or older on December 31, 2025 and own my Ontario home
I completed 200 or more hours as a volunteer firefighter or search and rescue volunteer

rent.message: Ontario renters may claim the Ontario Energy and Property Tax Credit component of the Ontario Trillium Benefit under OTA s.103.1. Eligible rent is the amount paid for a principal residence in Ontario. If you lived in subsidized housing, check with your landlord whether property tax was paid on the unit before entering the amount. Bring your landlord's name and address and the total rent paid in 2025.

document_checklist_add.document: Rent receipts or lease showing total rent paid in 2025 and landlord name and address

property_tax.message: Ontario homeowners who paid property tax directly may claim the Ontario Energy and Property Tax Credit under OTA s.103.1. Bring your 2025 property tax assessment notice and payment records.

document_checklist_add.document: 2025 Ontario property tax assessment notice showing annual property tax amount

senior_homeowner.message: The Ontario Senior Homeowners' Property Tax Grant under OTA s.104.1 provides up to $500 annually to homeowners who were 64 or older on December 31, 2025 and paid property tax on their Ontario principal residence. The full grant is available below $35,000 net income (single) or $45,000 (couple) and phases out to nil at $50,000 (single) or $60,000 (couple). It is claimed on Form ON-BEN filed with your T1 return. Your property tax documentation will cover this claim.

preparer_flag.flag: Assess Ontario Senior Homeowners' Property Tax Grant under OTA s.104.1 — confirm age 64+ on December 31, 2025, principal residence ownership, and net income threshold ($50,000 single / $60,000 couple)

volunteer.message: You may claim the Volunteer Firefighters Tax Credit or Search and Rescue Volunteers Tax Credit under ITA s.118.06 or s.118.07. The credit amount is $6,000, generating up to $900 in federal tax savings. You must have completed at least 200 hours of eligible service in 2025. Written certification from your fire department or search and rescue organization is required and must be kept in case CRA requests it.

document_checklist_add.document: Certification of volunteer service hours from fire department or search and rescue organization — confirms 200 or more eligible hours in 2025

preparer_flag.flag: Claim Volunteer Firefighters Tax Credit or Search and Rescue Volunteers Tax Credit under ITA s.118.06 or s.118.07 — $6,000 credit amount, 200-hour minimum confirmed by client

none.message: You may still qualify for the Ontario Sales Tax Credit component of the Ontario Trillium Benefit based on income alone, even without rent or property tax. Your preparer will assess this on Form ON-BEN.

screens.title: At any point in 2025, did the total cost of all foreign property you owned exceed $100,000 Canadian?
screens.instruction: If the total adjusted cost base of all specified foreign property exceeded $100,000 CAD at any point in 2025, you must file Form T1135 — Foreign Income Verification Statement. Failure to file carries significant penalties.

options.label: Yes — I owned foreign property worth over $100,000 CAD
I am not sure — I have foreign accounts or investments

true.message: Form T1135 — Foreign Income Verification Statement must be filed with your T1 return under ITA s.233.3. Specified foreign property includes foreign bank accounts, foreign stocks, foreign real estate (other than personal-use property), and interests in foreign entities. The penalty for failure to file is $25 per day up to $2,500, plus 5% of the cost of the unreported property for repeated failures. Do not miss this filing.

preparer_flag.flag: T1135 Foreign Income Verification Statement required — specified foreign property exceeded $100,000 CAD. Penalties are severe for failure to file.

unsure.message: If you have foreign bank accounts, investments, or real estate outside Canada, tally the cost (not current market value) of all holdings. If that total exceeded $100,000 Canadian at any point in 2025, T1135 is required. Your preparer will help you determine whether you must file.

preparer_flag.flag: Possible T1135 filing requirement — assess cost of all specified foreign property. Threshold is $100,000 CAD at any point in the year.

branch_completion_summary.message: Credits review complete. Your interview is now finished. As a reminder: the GST/HST Credit (ITA s.122.5) is a quarterly tax-free payment for low and moderate-income Canadians — it is assessed automatically when you file your T1. No separate application is required.
highlighted_documents: ON-BEN form information — rent or property tax amounts, senior homeowner eligibility if age 64 or older
T1135 if specified foreign property exceeded $100,000 CAD
Volunteer service certification if 200 or more hours completed
